canonical_definition: A suite of diagnostic tests evaluating six distinct syntactic phenomena through surprisal-based metrics, where models are assessed on their ability to assign appropriate surprisal to sentence continuations based on syntactic constraints.
papers: Follow the Beaten Path: The Role of Route Patterns on Vision-Language Navigation Agents Generalization Abilities
HIGGS: Pushing the Limits of Large Language Model Quantization via the Linearity Theorem
